name: Gangaramaya Temple
description: A prominent Buddhist temple in Colombo, known for its eclectic mix of modern architecture and cultural elements. It serves as a place of worship and a center for learning about Buddhism.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:30 AM) to avoid crowds and heat

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress modestly by covering your shoulders and knees, avoiding sleeveless tops, shorts, or revealing clothing; Remove hats/caps and shoes before entering, and consider wearing light colors like white as a sign of respect, with wraps often available if needed
behavior: Dress Code: Wear clothing that covers shoulders and knees. Sarongs are available at the entrance if necessary.
Footwear: Remove shoes and hats before entering the temple complex; a designated area is available for storage.
Decorum: Maintain silence, avoid loud talking, and do not touch religious artifacts or statues.
Photography: Allowed, but be considerate and avoid taking close-up shots of people without permission.
Offering/Donation: While entry is generally free, donations are appreciated, and a fee may be required for the museum area.
Cultural Respect: Be aware of the active, crowded nature of the temple and respectful of monks and devotees.

name: Galle Face Green
description: A popular ocean-side urban park in Colombo, known for its scenic views, recreational activities, and vibrant atmosphere. It serves as a gathering place for locals and tourists alike.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Evening (4:00 PM - 7:00 PM) to enjoy the sunset and cooler weather

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual and comfortable clothing is acceptable. Light clothing is recommended due to the heat. No strict dress code, but modest attire is advised
behavior: Public Decorum: Maintain respectful behavior as it is a family-friendly public space.
Cleanliness: Avoid littering; use designated bins to keep the beach clean.
Safety: Be cautious when near the ocean as swimming is generally not recommended due to strong currents.
Photography: Allowed freely; it is a popular location for sunset photography.
Food Vendors: Enjoy local street food but ensure cleanliness and hygiene.

name: Viharamahadevi Park
description: The oldest and largest public park in Colombo, located near the Town Hall. The park features landscaped gardens, walking paths, a large Buddha statue, fountains, and a children's play area, making it a peaceful place for relaxation and recreation.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:00 AM - 9:00 AM) or late afternoon (4:30 PM - 6:30 PM) to avoid heat

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual and comfortable clothing is acceptable. Light clothing is recommended due to warm weather. Modest attire is appreciated, especially near the Buddha statue.
behavior: Public Decorum: Maintain respectful behavior, especially near religious statues.
Cleanliness: Do not litter; use designated waste bins to keep the park clean.
Photography: Allowed, but be respectful when taking photos near the Buddha statue.
Family-Friendly Environment: The park is popular with families and children, so maintain appropriate behavior.
Safety: Follow park rules and supervise children in play areas.

name: National Museum of Colombo
description: The National Museum of Colombo is the largest museum in Sri Lanka, showcasing a vast collection of artifacts, including historical, cultural, and artistic treasures. It provides insights into the country's rich heritage and history.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (9:00 AM - 11:00 AM) to explore comfortably before peak hours

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual but modest clothing is recommended. Avoid overly revealing attire as it is a cultural institution.
behavior: Museum Etiquette: Maintain silence and avoid disturbing other visitors.
Photography: Photography may be restricted in certain sections; check for signs before taking photos.
Handling Artifacts: Do not touch exhibits unless clearly permitted.
Food and Drinks: Not allowed inside exhibition halls.
Respect: Follow instructions given by museum staff.

entry_fee: Paid entry (varies for locals and foreigners)
opening_hours: 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM (closed on Mondays and public holidays)

name: Kelaniya Raja Maha Vihara
description: Kelaniya Raja Maha Vihara is a Buddhist temple located in Kelaniya, near Colombo. It is renowned for its historical and religious significance, as well as its beautiful architecture and serene surroundings.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:00 AM - 9:00 AM) or late afternoon (4:30 PM - 6:30 PM) to avoid heat

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Wear modest clothing covering shoulders and knees. White or light-colored clothing is preferred. Remove shoes and hats before entering temple premises.
behavior: Respectful Conduct: Maintain silence and behave respectfully within the temple.
Footwear: Remove shoes before entering the shrine areas.
Photography: Allowed in certain areas, but avoid photographing monks or worshippers without permission.
Offerings: Visitors may bring flowers or incense as offerings.
Cultural Respect: Avoid turning your back directly to Buddha statues when taking photographs.

name: Mount Lavinia Beach
description: Mount Lavinia Beach is a popular beach destination in Colombo, known for its golden sands, vibrant atmosphere, and stunning sunsets. It is a great place for swimming, sunbathing, and enjoying beachside cafes and restaurants.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:00 AM - 9:00 AM) or late afternoon (4:30 PM - 6:30 PM) for cooler weather and to enjoy the sunset

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual beachwear is acceptable. Swimwear is allowed, but modest attire is recommended when leaving the beach area.
behavior: Safety: Swim only in designated safe areas and be cautious of strong currents.
Cleanliness: Avoid littering and help keep the beach clean.
Public Decorum: Maintain respectful behavior as it is a family-friendly location.
Photography: Allowed freely, especially for sunset photography.
Local Vendors: Purchase food and drinks from trusted vendors and check hygiene.

opening_hours: Open 24 hours (beach access), but swimming is recommended during daylight hours

name: Independence Memorial Hall (Independence Square)
description: A national monument built to commemorate Sri Lanka’s independence from British rule in 1948. Located in Independence Square, the site features a grand stone hall inspired by Kandyan architecture, landscaped gardens, and statues of national heroes, making it a popular place for relaxation, jogging, and photography.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:00 AM - 9:00 AM) or evening (4:30 PM - 7:00 PM) to avoid heat

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: casual and modest clothing is acceptable. Light clothing is recommended due to warm weather. Modest attire is appreciated, especially near the monument.
behavior: Respect: Maintain respectful behavior as it is a site of national importance.
Cleanliness: Do not litter; use waste bins provided.
Photography: Allowed freely; it is a popular spot for photos.
Public Use: The area is popular for jogging and walking; be mindful of others.
Monuments: Do not climb or damage statues and historical structures.

opening_hours: 24 hours (monument access), but visiting during daylight hours is recommended for safety and to enjoy the surroundings

name: Dehiwala Zoological Garden
description: Also known as the National Zoological Gardens of Sri Lanka, this zoo houses a wide variety of animals including mammals, birds, reptiles, and aquatic species. It is well known for its elephant shows, bird park, and educational programs promoting wildlife conservation.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (8:30 AM - 11:30 AM) before peak crowds and heat

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual and comfortable clothing is recommended. Light clothing and hats are advisable due to outdoor exposure.
behavior: Animal Safety: Do not feed or disturb animals unless permitted.
Cleanliness: Avoid littering and use designated waste bins.
Photography: Allowed, but avoid using flash around animals.
Respect Rules: Follow zoo guidelines and staff instructions.
Supervision: Children should be supervised at all times.

entry_fee: Paid entry (different rates for locals and foreigners)

name: Old Parliament Building
description: A historic colonial-era building located at the northern end of Galle Face Green. Opened in 1930, it originally housed the Parliament of Sri Lanka and now serves as the Presidential Secretariat. The building is known for its grand Neo-Baroque architecture and seaside location.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Evening (4:30 PM - 6:30 PM) when visiting Galle Face Green nearby

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual but modest clothing is recommended as it is a government building area.
behavior: Security: The building is not open to the public; visitors can view it from outside only.
Photography: Allowed from public areas, but avoid restricted zones.
Respect: Maintain respectful behavior as it is an important government institution.
Cleanliness: Avoid littering around the premises.

name: Jami Ul-Alfar Mosque
description: Also known as the Red Mosque, this iconic landmark in the Pettah area of Colombo is famous for its striking red-and-white candy-striped architecture. Built in 1908, it serves as an important place of worship for the Muslim community and is one of the most photographed buildings in the city.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Outside prayer times, preferably morning (9:00 AM - 11:00 AM)

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Modest clothing required. Shoulders and knees must be covered. Women may be required to cover their heads. Shoes must be removed before entering.
behavior: Prayer Respect: Avoid visiting during prayer times unless you are participating.
Footwear: Remove shoes before entering the mosque.
Photography: Ask permission before taking photos inside; exterior photography is allowed.
Silence: Maintain quiet and respectful behavior.
Gender Areas: Follow any designated prayer area guidelines.

name: Sri Lanka Planetarium
description: Established in 1965, this is the first and only planetarium in Sri Lanka. It is famous for its unique lotus-shaped architecture and serves as a major hub for astronomy education, featuring a 4D/Digital full-dome projector that displays celestial events and shows.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Weekday mornings; show times are typically at 10:00 AM and 2:00 PM

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual and comfortable clothing is appropriate; however, avoid extremely revealing attire as a sign of general cultural respect in public institutions.
behavior: Punctuality: Arrive at least 15-30 minutes before show times (10 AM and 2 PM) as late entry is strictly prohibited once the dome is dark.
Noise Control: Maintain silence inside the auditorium to ensure everyone can hear the lecture and enjoy the immersive experience.
Food & Drink: No outside food or beverages are allowed inside the dome or the exhibition areas.
Electronics: Use of mobile phones is discouraged during the show; the bright light from screens disrupts the dark-sky environment.
Photography: Photography is generally allowed in the gardens and exhibition areas, but flash photography is strictly prohibited inside the auditorium during shows.
Group Visits: School groups are very common; be prepared for high energy and large crowds during term time.
entry_requirements: Closed on Sundays, Mondays, and Public Holidays

entry_fee: Rs. 100 for Adults, Rs. 50 for Children/Students
opening_hours: 8.00 AM - 4.00 PM (Shows at 10.00 AM and 2.00 PM)

name: Beddagana Wetland Park
description: A protected urban wetland sanctuary located in Sri Jayawardenepura Kotte. The park is known for its rich biodiversity, including migratory birds, butterflies, reptiles, and small mammals. Wooden boardwalks allow visitors to explore the wetlands while enjoying nature and birdwatching.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:00 AM - 9:00 AM) for birdwatching and cooler weather

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Wear comfortable and modest outdoor clothing. Light, breathable fabrics and comfortable walking shoes are recommended.
behavior: Wildlife Protection: Do not disturb or feed animals and birds.
Silence: Maintain quiet behavior to avoid disturbing wildlife.
Cleanliness: Do not litter; help preserve the natural environment.
Photography: Allowed, especially for nature photography, but avoid disturbing animals.
Stay on Paths: Use designated boardwalks and trails only.

entry_fee: Paid entry (affordable rates for locals and foreigners)

name: Attidiya Bird Sanctuary
description: A shallow freshwater wetland located in the urban sprawl of Colombo. It is a critical habitat for over 50 species of migratory and resident birds, as well as butterflies and several endemic freshwater fish. It offers a tranquil escape for birdwatching and nature photography.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:00 AM - 8:00 AM) or late afternoon (4:30 PM - 6:00 PM) for peak bird activity.

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Wear comfortable, earth-toned or neutral-colored clothing to blend into the environment; sturdy walking shoes or boots are recommended as paths can be muddy.
behavior: Silence: Keep noise to a minimum to avoid startling the birds and wildlife.
Environmental Care: Strictly no littering; ensure all plastic and waste are carried back out with you.
Wildlife Respect: Maintain a safe distance from animals and do not attempt to feed the birds or crocodiles.
Safety: Be cautious of the marshy terrain and stay on designated paths; crocodiles are known to inhabit these waters.
Equipment: Bring binoculars and insect repellent, as mosquitoes are common in the wetland.
Photography: Nature photography is encouraged, but avoid using drones without specific prior authorization.

opening_hours: Open 24 hours (Recommended during daylight: 6:00 AM - 6:00 PM)

name: Diyatha Uyana Park
description: A beautifully landscaped urban park and lakeside walking path located at Polduwa Junction. It features a water fountain, an aquarium, a floating restaurant, and a popular plant nursery. It serves as a hub for recreation, fitness, and weekend markets.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Late afternoon (5:00 PM onwards) to enjoy the cooler breeze and the illuminated fountains.

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual or sportswear is recommended for walking and jogging; modest casual wear is appropriate for the food court and markets.
behavior: Public Conduct: A family-friendly environment; avoid loud music and public displays of affection that may be considered inappropriate in local culture.
Environmental Care: Strictly no littering; the park is well-maintained with numerous bins provided.
Pet Policy: Generally, pets are not allowed in the main walking and seated areas to maintain cleanliness.
Market Etiquette: On weekends, support local vendors at the Good Market or plant stalls; polite bargaining is acceptable but not always expected.
Safety: Be cautious near the edge of the Diyawanna Lake, especially with children, as there are no high fences.
Cycling/Skating: Restricted in certain high-traffic walking areas to ensure pedestrian safety.

name: St. Anthony's Shrine
description: A historic Roman Catholic shrine and national monument located in Kochchikade. Dedicated to St. Anthony of Padua, it is world-renowned for its reported miracles and houses a sacred relic (a piece of the Saint's tongue). It remains a deeply spiritual site for Catholics, Hindus, Buddhists, and Muslims alike.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning on weekdays; note that Tuesdays are exceptionally crowded due to the weekly Novena.

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress modestly by covering shoulders and knees. While footwear removal is not required inside the church (unlike temples), many devotees choose to enter barefoot as a sign of penance.
behavior: Veneration: You will see many people touching the glass casing of the St. Anthony statue at the entrance; please be respectful of this ritual and wait your turn.
Candle Lighting: It is a tradition to light yellow candles at the designated outdoor trench; ensure you purchase candles from the authorized church shop.
Mass Decorum: If a service (Mass) is in progress, remain silent and avoid walking near the altar or taking photos of the congregation.
Silence & Prayer: The interior is a space for deep prayer; keep your voice to a whisper even when exploring the architecture.
Photography: Generally allowed, but strictly prohibited during Mass and religious ceremonies. Avoid using flash inside the shrine.
Respect for History: Acknowledge the shrine's significance as a site of resilience following the 2019 Easter attacks; maintain a somber and respectful attitude.

name: Henarathgoda Botanical Garden
description: Established in 1876, this historic garden is famous for housing the first rubber tree planted in Sri Lanka. It features a diverse collection of tropical flora, extensive lawns, and a scenic lake, serving as both a botanical research center and a popular recreational spot.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (7:30 AM - 9:30 AM) to enjoy the birdlife and avoid the midday humidity.

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual and comfortable outdoor wear; comfortable walking shoes are highly recommended for exploring the large grounds.
behavior: Nature Conservation: Strictly no plucking of flowers, damaging plants, or disturbing the wildlife.
Environmental Care: Maintain cleanliness and use designated bins; avoid bringing single-use plastics into the garden.
Recreation: Popular for picnics and pre-wedding photography; be mindful of other visitors' space and privacy.
Safety: Stay on the marked paths and be cautious near the lakeside areas.
Quietude: Respect the peaceful environment by avoiding loud music or disruptive activities.
Historical Respect: Visit the site of the original rubber tree, which is a significant landmark in the country's economic history.
entry_requirements: Photography permitted (additional fees may apply for professional shoots)

entry_fee: Rs. 100 for Adults, Rs. 50 for Children (Local); Higher rates apply for foreign visitors

name: National Basilica of Our Lady of Lanka
description: A major Roman Catholic pilgrimage site in Sri Lanka, built as a thanksgiving offering after World War II. It is known for its peaceful atmosphere and annual feast celebrations.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (7:00 AM - 10:00 AM)

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Modest clothing required covering shoulders and knees.
behavior: Maintain silence during prayers.
Photography allowed outside; ask before taking photos inside.
Respect clergy and worshippers.

name: St. Mary's Church
description: One of the largest cathedrals in Sri Lanka, located in Grand Street, Negombo. It features beautiful ceiling paintings and impressive architecture.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning hours outside mass times

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Modest attire required.
behavior: Avoid visiting during mass unless attending.
Maintain silence inside.
Photography allowed without flash.

name: Water World Kelaniya
description: Sri Lanka’s first public aquarium and underwater tunnel experience. It features freshwater and marine species along with educational exhibits.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (9:00 AM - 11:00 AM)

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual comfortable clothing.
behavior: Do not tap on aquarium glass.
Follow staff instructions.
Supervise children.

name: Guruge Nature Park
description: A family-friendly theme and cultural park featuring replicas of Sri Lankan heritage sites, small zoo areas, and entertainment facilities.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning or late afternoon

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual comfortable clothing.
behavior: Follow park rules.
Do not disturb animals.
Keep environment clean.

name: Negombo Beach
description: A popular coastal destination known for golden sands, fishing boats, beachside hotels, and stunning sunsets.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Evening (4:30 PM - sunset)

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Beachwear allowed; modest attire outside beach area.
behavior: Avoid swimming in rough sea conditions.
Do not litter.
Be respectful in public spaces.

name: Horagolla National Park
description: A small national park known for secondary forest vegetation and wildlife including monkeys, birds, and deer.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (6:00 AM - 9:00 AM)

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Outdoor comfortable clothing.
behavior: Do not feed wildlife.
Stay on designated paths.
Follow park regulations.

name: Negombo Lagoon
description: A large estuarine lagoon famous for fishing, boat rides, mangroves, and birdwatching.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning or sunset

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual comfortable clothing.
behavior: Use life jackets during boat rides.
Avoid polluting water.
Respect local fishermen.
entry_requirements: Boat rides available at extra cost

name: Richmond Castle
description: A grand Edwardian mansion built in the early 20th century, showcasing colonial architecture and beautiful gardens. It is one of the most famous heritage mansions in Sri Lanka.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (9:00 AM - 11:00 AM)

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual but modest clothing recommended.
behavior: Do not touch antique furniture or artifacts.
Follow staff instructions.
Photography allowed in permitted areas.

name: Kalutara Bodhiya
description: A sacred Buddhist temple located along the Kalu River. It is famous for its hollow stupa where visitors can enter and worship.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:00 AM - 9:00 AM)

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: White or modest clothing covering shoulders and knees.
behavior: Remove shoes before entering.
Maintain silence.
Do not turn your back directly to Buddha statues for photos.

name: Calido Beach
description: A peaceful beach located near the mouth of the Kalu River. It offers scenic views where the river meets the sea.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Evening (4:30 PM - sunset)

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Beachwear allowed; modest attire outside beach area.
behavior: Be cautious of strong currents.
Avoid littering.
Follow local safety signs.

name: Kalido Public Beach
description: A public beach area popular among locals for relaxation, swimming, and enjoying sunsets.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Late afternoon (4:30 PM - 6:30 PM)

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual beachwear acceptable.
behavior: Respect families and other visitors.
Keep the beach clean.
Avoid swimming during rough sea conditions.

name: Kalutara Bridge
description: A major bridge crossing the Kalu River, connecting Kalutara town. It offers scenic river views and is a key landmark in the area.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Sunset for scenic river views

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual attire.
behavior: Follow traffic and pedestrian safety rules.
Avoid standing in restricted areas.

name: Thudugala Ella
description: A scenic waterfall located in a tea estate area, surrounded by lush greenery. It is a popular eco-tourism destination.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (8:00 AM - 11:00 AM)

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Comfortable outdoor clothing.
behavior: Avoid littering.
Be cautious on slippery rocks.
Respect local estate property.

name: Thudugala Waterfall
description: Also known as Thudugala Ella, this natural waterfall is surrounded by tea plantations and is ideal for nature lovers and photographers.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (8:00 AM - 11:00 AM)

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Outdoor comfortable clothing.
behavior: Be careful of slippery surfaces.
Do not pollute the water.
Follow eco-tourism rules.

name: Koneswaram Temple
description: A historic Hindu temple perched on Swami Rock overlooking the Indian Ocean, Koneswaram is one of the Pancha Ishwarams dedicated to Lord Shiva.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning or evening

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Modest attire required
behavior: Remove footwear
Photography restricted in inner shrine

name: Nallur Kandaswamy Kovil
description: One of the most significant Hindu temples in Sri Lanka, dedicated to Lord Murugan and known for its grand annual festival.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Strictly modest clothing
behavior: Remove footwear
No photography inside sanctum

name: Jaffna Fort
description: A massive Dutch-built fort showcasing colonial military architecture and views of the Jaffna lagoon.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Evening

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual attire
behavior: Do not damage structures
Follow marked paths

name: Keerimalai Naguleswaram Temple
description: An ancient Hindu temple near natural springs believed to have healing properties, overlooking the Indian Ocean.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Modest clothing
behavior: Remove footwear
Bathing allowed in designated areas only

name: Casuarina Beach
description: One of the safest swimming beaches in northern Sri Lanka, known for shallow waters and white sand.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Beachwear acceptable
behavior: No littering
Swim only in safe areas

name: Delft Island (Neduntheevu)
description: A remote island famous for wild ponies, coral walls, and Dutch-era ruins, offering a unique rural island experience.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Day trip (morning ferry)

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual attire
behavior: Do not disturb animals
Carry drinking water

name: Nagadeepa Rajamaha Viharaya
description: One of the 16 sacred Buddhist sites in Sri Lanka, believed to be visited by the Buddha during his second visit.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: White or modest attire
behavior: Remove footwear
Maintain silence

name: Mannar Fort
description: A well-preserved Dutch fort located on Mannar Island, reflecting colonial history and coastal defense.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Late afternoon

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual attire
behavior: No vandalism
Respect heritage site

name: Adam’s Bridge (Rama Setu)
description: A chain of limestone shoals connecting Sri Lanka to India, visible via satellite and rich in mythological significance.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Daytime (viewpoints only)

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual attire
behavior: Access restricted to viewpoints
No swimming

name: Vankalai Bird Sanctuary
description: A Ramsar wetland site famous for migratory birds, mangroves, and coastal ecosystems.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Neutral colors
behavior: No loud noise
No littering

name: Kandarodai Archaeological Site
description: An ancient Buddhist archaeological site featuring small stupas dating back to the Anuradhapura period, reflecting early Buddhist influence in the north.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Modest attire
behavior: Remove footwear near stupas
Do not climb structures

name: Nilavarai Bottomless Well
description: A mysterious freshwater well believed to never dry up, associated with ancient legends and local folklore.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Daytime

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual attire
behavior: No littering
Do not attempt to enter the well

name: Point Pedro Lighthouse
description: Located at the northernmost point of Sri Lanka, this lighthouse offers views of the Indian Ocean and surrounding coastal landscape.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Evening

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual attire
behavior: Restricted access inside lighthouse
Photography allowed outside

name: Chundikulam National Park
description: A coastal national park and bird sanctuary known for lagoons, migratory birds, and mangrove ecosystems.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Neutral colors recommended
behavior: Do not disturb birds
No loud noises

name: Elephant Pass War Memorial
description: A memorial site marking the strategic Elephant Pass, symbolizing a significant location in Sri Lanka’s modern history.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Daytime

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Respectful attire
behavior: Maintain decorum
No vandalism

name: Thiruketheeswaram Temple
description: One of the Pancha Ishwarams dedicated to Lord Shiva, with deep religious significance and ancient origins.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Strictly modest attire
behavior: Remove footwear
No photography in sanctum

name: Giant’s Tank (Yoda Wewa)
description: An ancient irrigation reservoir built during the Anuradhapura era, still supporting agriculture and wildlife.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Evening

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual attire
behavior: Do not enter water
Respect local farmers

name: Iranamadu Tank
description: One of Sri Lanka’s largest reservoirs, supplying water to the Northern Province and surrounded by scenic landscapes.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Evening

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual attire
behavior: No swimming
Fishing only with permission

name: Mullaitivu Beach Park
description: A peaceful coastal park area ideal for relaxation, evening walks, and enjoying sea views in Mullaitivu town.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Evening

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Modest casual wear
behavior: Keep the park clean
Avoid swimming during rough seas

name: Vavuniya Archaeological Site
description: A small archaeological area highlighting ancient ruins and early settlement evidence in the Vavuniya region.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual attire
behavior: Do not disturb ruins
Follow local guidance

name: Kantharodai Archaeological Museum
description: A small museum displaying artifacts discovered from the Kantharodai archaeological area, providing insight into early northern civilizations.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Mid-morning

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Modest attire
behavior: No touching exhibits
Photography limited

name: Sri Dalada Maligawa
description: The Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic is one of the most sacred Buddhist temples in Sri Lanka, housing the relic of the tooth of the Buddha. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a major pilgrimage destination.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:00 AM) during the first Tevava (worship session) to avoid large crowds

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress modestly by covering shoulders and knees; white clothing is preferred as a sign of respect. Sarongs are available at the entrance if needed. Remove shoes and hats before entering.
behavior: Dress Code: White or light-colored clothing is preferred. Shoulders and knees must be covered at all times.
Footwear: Remove shoes before entering the temple; a designated area is provided for storage.
Decorum: Maintain silence and reverence throughout the temple. Avoid turning your back to the relic chamber.
Photography: Photography is restricted inside the inner shrine room. Respect all signage regarding camera use.
Queuing: Follow the designated queue systems during worship sessions (Tevava), which occur three times daily.
Cultural Respect: Bow respectfully when passing monks and devotees. Avoid pointing feet toward the shrine.

name: Udawatta Kele Sanctuary
description: A scenic forest reserve located just above the Kandy city center, historically used as a forbidden forest by Kandyan kings. It is home to a rich variety of flora, fauna, and birdlife, offering serene walking trails through the jungle.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (7:00 AM - 9:00 AM) for birdwatching and cooler temperatures

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Wear comfortable, light clothing suitable for walking. Closed-toe shoes or hiking sandals are recommended for the trails.
behavior: Wildlife: Do not disturb or feed wildlife. Maintain a respectful distance from animals.
Trails: Stick to designated trails and avoid venturing off-path.
Littering: Do not litter; carry out all waste to keep the sanctuary clean.
Noise: Keep noise levels low to avoid disturbing wildlife and other visitors.
Photography: Photography of nature and wildlife is encouraged; avoid flash photography near animals.
Guided Tours: Hiring a local guide is recommended for a richer experience and better navigation.
entry_requirements: Guided tours available at the entrance

name: Royal Botanic Gardens, Peradeniya
description: One of the finest botanical gardens in Asia, spanning over 147 acres along the Mahaweli River. The gardens feature an extraordinary collection of orchids, palms, medicinal plants, and a famous avenue of royal palms, attracting nature lovers and researchers alike.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Weekday mornings (8:00 AM - 11:00 AM) to enjoy the gardens before crowds and midday heat arrive

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual, comfortable clothing suitable for walking outdoors. Sunscreen and a hat are recommended.
behavior: Plants: Do not pluck, damage, or remove any plants, flowers, or fruits from the garden.
Littering: Strictly no littering; bins are available throughout the garden.
Picnicking: Picnicking is allowed in designated areas only.
Pets: Pets are not permitted inside the gardens.
Photography: Photography is freely allowed for personal use throughout the gardens.
Vehicles: Bicycles and vehicles are restricted to specific paths only.

name: Kandy Viewpoint
description: A popular hilltop vantage point offering breathtaking panoramic views of the Kandy city skyline, Kandy Lake, and the surrounding misty hills. It is especially stunning at sunrise and sunset, making it a favorite spot for photographers and tourists.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning or late afternoon (5:30 PM) for golden hour views over the city

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual clothing is appropriate. Comfortable shoes are recommended for the uphill walk.
behavior: Littering: Do not litter; help keep the viewpoint clean for all visitors.
Noise: Be respectful of other visitors enjoying the view; keep noise to a minimum.
Photography: Photography is freely allowed and highly encouraged from all angles.
Safety: Stay within safe boundaries and avoid climbing on rocks or barriers.
Vendors: Local vendors operate nearby; engage respectfully if purchasing souvenirs or refreshments.

name: Ceylon Tea Museum
description: Housed in a restored colonial-era tea factory in Hantane, the Ceylon Tea Museum showcases the rich history of Sri Lanka's famous tea industry. Exhibits include antique tea processing machinery, historical photographs, and interactive displays tracing the journey from leaf to cup.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Midmorning (10:00 AM - 12:00 PM) on weekdays for a quieter and more relaxed visit

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Smart casual clothing is appropriate. Comfortable shoes are recommended as the museum spans multiple floors.
behavior: Exhibits: Do not touch or tamper with machinery and historical exhibits on display.
Photography: Photography is generally allowed; check for any restricted zones inside.
Guided Tours: Guided tours are available and highly recommended to fully appreciate the exhibits.
Tea Tasting: Take advantage of the tea tasting session offered at the museum for an authentic experience.
Noise: Maintain a respectful noise level inside exhibit halls.
Purchasing: A gift shop is available for purchasing authentic Ceylon tea and souvenirs.

name: Kandy Lake
description: An artificial lake built by the last Kandyan king, Sri Wickrama Rajasinha, in 1807, located in the heart of Kandy city. The lake is surrounded by a scenic walking path and offers tranquil views of the surrounding hills and the Sri Dalada Maligawa, making it a beloved spot for locals and visitors alike.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:00 AM - 8:00 AM) for a peaceful lakeside walk before the city comes alive

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual clothing suitable for walking. Comfortable footwear is recommended for the lakeside path.
behavior: Littering: Strictly no littering around or into the lake; help maintain the cleanliness of this historic site.
Swimming: Swimming in the lake is strictly prohibited.
Wildlife: Do not disturb or feed the birds and monitor lizards that inhabit the lake area.
Photography: Photography is freely allowed throughout the lakeside area.
Noise: Be respectful of local residents and other visitors enjoying the tranquil atmosphere.
Vendors: Street food vendors and boat ride services operate near the lake; engage respectfully.

name: Commonwealth War Cemetery Kandy
description: A beautifully maintained cemetery administered by the Commonwealth War Graves Commission, honoring the memory of over 200 Allied servicemen who died in Sri Lanka during World War II. The site is characterized by immaculate rows of white headstones set within serene, manicured gardens.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (8:00 AM - 11:00 AM) for a quiet and reflective visit

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress modestly and respectfully out of reverence for those interred. Avoid casual or revealing clothing.
behavior: Respect: Maintain a solemn and respectful demeanor throughout the cemetery at all times.
Noise: Keep all noise to an absolute minimum; this is a place of remembrance and reflection.
Headstones: Do not touch, lean on, or damage any headstones or memorial structures.
Photography: Photography is permitted but must be done respectfully and discreetly.
Littering: No littering of any kind is permitted within the cemetery grounds.
Flowers: Laying flowers as a tribute is welcome and encouraged as a sign of respect.

name: International Buddhist Museum
description: Located within the Sri Dalada Maligawa complex, the International Buddhist Museum showcases Buddhist art, artifacts, and traditions from countries across Asia and beyond. Each gallery represents a different Buddhist nation, offering a comprehensive journey through global Buddhist heritage and culture.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Weekday mornings (9:00 AM - 11:00 AM) for a quieter and more immersive experience

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress modestly with shoulders and knees covered. White or light-colored clothing is preferred as a sign of respect. Remove shoes before entering.
behavior: Dress Code: Modest attire covering shoulders and knees is mandatory. Sarongs may be available if needed.
Footwear: Remove shoes at the entrance before entering the museum galleries.
Artifacts: Do not touch or handle any artifacts or religious items on display.
Photography: Photography is allowed in most areas; follow signage regarding restricted zones.
Noise: Maintain a respectful and quiet demeanor throughout the museum.
Cultural Respect: Be mindful that the museum is situated within a sacred temple complex.

entry_fee: Included with Sri Dalada Maligawa ticket

name: Old Royal Palace
description: The historic royal palace of the Kandyan kings, located adjacent to the Sri Dalada Maligawa. The palace complex served as the seat of the last Sinhalese kingdom and is a significant landmark of Sri Lanka's royal and architectural heritage, blending Kandyan and South Indian architectural styles.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning hours (8:30 AM - 11:00 AM) on weekdays to explore the complex with fewer visitors

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress modestly and respectfully given the historical and cultural significance of the site. Comfortable walking shoes are recommended.
behavior: Respect: Treat the historical structures with great care and avoid touching or leaning on ancient walls.
Photography: Photography is generally allowed; be mindful of any restricted areas.
Noise: Maintain a respectful noise level throughout the palace complex.
Guided Tours: Engaging a guide is recommended to fully understand the historical significance of the site.
Littering: No littering of any kind within the palace grounds.
Cultural Sensitivity: Be aware of the deep cultural and historical importance of this royal site to Sri Lankan heritage.

entry_fee: Included with Sri Dalada Maligawa complex ticket

name: Kandy National Museum
description: Housed in a beautifully preserved colonial-era building within the former royal palace complex, the Kandy National Museum displays an extensive collection of Kandyan royal regalia, jewelry, traditional costumes, weapons, and artifacts that chronicle the glory of the last Sinhalese kingdom.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Weekday mornings (9:00 AM - 11:30 AM) for a calm and unhurried experience

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Smart casual attire is appropriate. Comfortable footwear is recommended as the museum spans several rooms.
behavior: Exhibits: Do not touch or handle any artifacts or display items within the museum.
Photography: Photography may be restricted inside; check with museum staff before taking pictures.
Noise: Maintain a quiet and respectful atmosphere throughout the museum.
Guided Tours: Audio guides or guided tours may be available and are highly recommended.
Littering: No food or drinks are permitted inside the museum galleries.
Children: Supervise children at all times to ensure the safety of exhibits.

name: Pallekele International Cricket Stadium
description: One of Sri Lanka's premier international cricket venues, set against the stunning backdrop of lush green hills on the outskirts of Kandy. The stadium hosts international Test matches, ODIs, and T20 games and is renowned for its scenic beauty and passionate local crowd atmosphere.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Match days for the full atmosphere; non-match days are ideal for a quiet stadium tour

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual clothing is appropriate. Wear comfortable footwear suitable for stadium seating and walking.
behavior: Match Days: Follow all security and entry protocols on match days, including bag checks and restricted items.
Ticketing: Purchase tickets in advance for international matches as they sell out quickly.
Behavior: Maintain sportsmanlike conduct and avoid disruptive or offensive behavior in the stands.
Photography: Photography for personal use is generally allowed; professional equipment may require accreditation.
Food & Drink: Outside food and beverages may be restricted on match days; check event-specific rules.
Respect: Be respectful of players, officials, and fellow spectators at all times.
entry_requirements: Match day tickets required for games
Stadium tours may be available on non-match days

entry_fee: Varies by match and seating category
opening_hours: Match dependent; Stadium open during scheduled events

name: British Garrison Cemetery
description: A historic cemetery established during British colonial rule, containing the graves of British soldiers, civil servants, and their families from the 19th century. The site is maintained in a serene and dignified manner and offers a poignant glimpse into Kandy's colonial past and the lives of those who served the British Empire in Ceylon.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (8:00 AM - 11:00 AM) for a peaceful and reflective visit

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress modestly and respectfully. Avoid casual or revealing clothing out of reverence for the site.
behavior: Respect: Maintain a solemn and quiet demeanor throughout the cemetery at all times.
Headstones: Do not touch, sit on, or damage any headstones or memorial structures.
Photography: Photography is permitted but should be done with sensitivity and discretion.
Noise: Keep all noise to an absolute minimum as a sign of respect for those interred.
Littering: No littering of any kind is permitted within the cemetery grounds.
Guided Information: A caretaker may be present who can share historical information about notable graves.

name: Sri Lankathilake Rajamaha Viharaya
description: A magnificent 14th-century Buddhist and Hindu temple built by King Bhuvanekabahu IV, perched dramatically on a rocky outcrop near Kandy. The temple blends Sinhalese, South Indian, and Gothic architectural styles and is dedicated to both Buddhist and Hindu deities, reflecting the syncretic religious traditions of medieval Sri Lanka.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (7:00 AM - 9:00 AM) to experience the temple in peaceful tranquility before visitors arrive

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress modestly with shoulders and knees fully covered. Remove shoes and hats before entering the temple. White or light-colored clothing is preferred.
behavior: Dress Code: Modest attire is mandatory. Sarongs may be available at the entrance if needed.
Footwear: Remove shoes before climbing the steps and entering the temple premises.
Decorum: Maintain silence and reverence within the temple. Avoid loud conversation or disruptive behavior.
Photography: Photography is generally allowed outside; restrictions may apply inside the shrine rooms.
Offerings: Floral offerings and incense may be purchased nearby and offered respectfully.
Cultural Respect: Be mindful of the dual Buddhist and Hindu significance of this ancient site.

name: Royal Palace Park
description: A beautifully landscaped public park adjacent to the historic Kandy royal palace complex and Kandy Lake. The park is a beloved green retreat in the heart of the city, offering manicured lawns, ornamental plants, and shaded pathways frequented by locals and tourists seeking rest and recreation.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:00 AM - 8:00 AM) or late afternoon (4:00 PM - 6:00 PM) for a pleasant and crowd-free experience

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual, comfortable clothing suitable for outdoor walking. No specific dress code required.
behavior: Littering: Strictly no littering; use designated bins to dispose of waste.
Plants: Do not pluck, damage, or remove any plants or flowers from the park.
Noise: Be respectful of other visitors and keep noise at a reasonable level.
Photography: Photography is freely allowed throughout the park.
Pets: Check local regulations regarding pets before visiting.
Respect: Be considerate of local families and elderly visitors using the park for relaxation.

name: St Paul's Church
description: One of the oldest Anglican churches in Kandy, built during the British colonial period in the early 19th century. St Paul's Church is known for its elegant colonial architecture, beautiful stained glass windows, and well-maintained cemetery. It continues to serve as an active place of worship for the local Anglican community.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Weekday mornings (9:00 AM - 11:00 AM) for a quiet visit; Sunday services offer a unique cultural experience

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress modestly and respectfully. Avoid casual or revealing clothing as the church is an active place of worship.
behavior: Dress Code: Modest attire is required at all times. Shoulders should be covered.
Silence: Maintain silence and respectful behavior, especially during services.
Photography: Photography is generally permitted outside; seek permission before photographing inside during services.
Worship: Be respectful of ongoing services and avoid disrupting worshippers.
Cemetery: Treat the adjoining cemetery with respect and solemnity.
Visits During Services: If visiting during a service, participate quietly or wait until after for a full tour.

name: Red Mosque of Kandy
description: Officially known as the Meera Makkam Mosque, the Red Mosque is a striking and iconic landmark in the heart of Kandy city, easily recognized by its vibrant red and white striped facade and Moorish architectural style. It is one of the most prominent mosques in Kandy and an active center of worship for the local Muslim community.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Mid-morning (10:00 AM - 12:00 PM) on non-Friday days to avoid peak prayer times

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress very modestly. Women must cover their head, arms, and legs fully. Men should wear long trousers and avoid sleeveless tops. Remove shoes before entering.
behavior: Dress Code: Full modest dress is mandatory. Women are required to cover their hair; headscarves may be available at the entrance.
Footwear: Remove shoes before entering the mosque premises.
Prayer Times: Avoid visiting during the five daily prayer times, especially Friday midday prayers.
Photography: Seek explicit permission before photographing inside or around the mosque.
Silence: Maintain complete silence and respectful behavior inside the mosque.
Cultural Sensitivity: Be aware of the active religious significance of the mosque and act accordingly at all times.

opening_hours: Open daily; avoid visiting during prayer times

name: Sigiriya
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of Sri Lanka's most iconic landmarks, Sigiriya is an ancient rock fortress rising 200 meters above the surrounding jungle. Built by King Kashyapa in the 5th century AD, it features remarkable frescoes, the famous Mirror Wall, ornamental gardens, and a breathtaking palace complex at the summit, offering panoramic views of the surrounding countryside.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (7:00 AM opening) to complete the climb before the midday heat and to enjoy the summit with fewer crowds

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Wear light, breathable clothing and sturdy closed-toe shoes suitable for a steep rock climb. Carry sun protection including a hat and sunscreen.
behavior: Climbing: The ascent involves steep metal staircases and uneven rock surfaces; those with vertigo or mobility issues should assess carefully.
Frescoes: Do not touch the ancient frescoes on the rock face; this is strictly prohibited and enforced.
Mirror Wall: Writing or touching the Mirror Wall is strictly prohibited. It is a protected historical artifact.
Photography: Photography is allowed throughout the site; no flash photography near the frescoes.
Hydration: Carry sufficient water as the climb can be physically demanding, especially in warm weather.
Wildlife: Be aware of hornets' nests near the frescoes area; follow guide instructions carefully.
entry_requirements: No touching of frescoes or Mirror Wall

name: Sri Natha Devalaya
description: One of the four main devalayas (shrines) within the Kandy royal complex, Sri Natha Devalaya is dedicated to the Bodhisattva Maitreya (the future Buddha), locally venerated as Natha. It is considered the oldest surviving structure in Kandy, predating the Dalada Maligawa, and is a place of great spiritual significance for both Buddhists and Hindus.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:30 AM - 8:30 AM) during puja (offering) sessions for an authentic spiritual experience

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress modestly with shoulders and knees covered. Remove shoes before entering the shrine. White or light-colored clothing is preferred.
behavior: Dress Code: Modest attire covering shoulders and knees is mandatory at all times.
Footwear: Remove shoes before entering the devalaya premises.
Decorum: Maintain silence and deep respect throughout the shrine complex.
Photography: Photography may be restricted inside the shrine room; follow posted signage.
Offerings: Floral offerings and incense are appropriate; follow the customs of other devotees.
Cultural Respect: This is an active place of worship; be mindful of devotees engaged in prayer and ritual.

name: Ambuluwawa Biodiversity Complex
description: A unique multi-religious and biodiversity complex situated atop the Ambuluwawa mountain peak near Gampola. The site features a remarkable conical tower offering panoramic views across four provinces, as well as Buddhist, Hindu, Muslim, and Christian religious structures harmoniously coexisting within a lush natural forest reserve rich in biodiversity.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (7:00 AM - 9:00 AM) on clear days for the best panoramic views from the tower summit

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Wear comfortable clothing and sturdy shoes suitable for uphill walking. Dress modestly when visiting the religious shrines within the complex.
behavior: Tower Climb: The spiral tower staircase is narrow and steep; those with vertigo should exercise caution.
Religious Sites: Dress modestly and remove footwear when visiting any of the religious shrines on-site.
Wildlife: Do not disturb wildlife or damage vegetation within the biodiversity forest reserve.
Littering: Strictly no littering; carry all waste out of the nature reserve.
Photography: Photography is freely allowed throughout the complex and offers spectacular views.
Respect: Honor the multi-religious nature of the complex by being respectful of all faith traditions represented.

name: Ranawana Purana Rajamaha Viharaya
description: An ancient and revered Buddhist temple nestled in the countryside near Kandy, known for its historical significance and serene natural surroundings. The temple features traditional Kandyan architectural elements, ancient murals, and a deeply spiritual atmosphere that draws pilgrims and visitors seeking peace and cultural heritage.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:30 AM - 9:00 AM) during morning puja for an authentic and peaceful visit

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress modestly with shoulders and knees fully covered. White clothing is preferred as a mark of respect. Remove shoes and hats before entering.
behavior: Dress Code: Modest white or light-colored attire is preferred. Sarongs may be available at the entrance.
Footwear: Remove shoes before entering the temple premises.
Decorum: Maintain complete silence and reverence inside the temple.
Photography: Seek permission from temple authorities before photographing murals or sacred areas.
Offerings: Floral and lamp offerings are welcome and can typically be purchased nearby.
Cultural Respect: Be mindful of monks and devotees engaged in worship and do not interrupt rituals.

name: Sri Vishnu Devala
description: One of the four principal devalayas (shrines) of the Kandy royal complex, Sri Vishnu Devala is dedicated to the Hindu deity Vishnu, who is revered in Sri Lanka as the guardian deity of the island. The shrine is an important pilgrimage destination for both Hindus and Buddhists and plays a central role in the annual Kandy Esala Perahera procession.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:30 AM - 8:30 AM) during puja sessions for an authentic devotional experience

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress modestly with shoulders and knees fully covered. Remove shoes before entering. White or light-colored clothing is appropriate.
behavior: Dress Code: Modest attire is mandatory. Sarongs may be provided at the entrance if needed.
Footwear: Remove shoes before entering the devalaya premises.
Decorum: Maintain silence and deep respect inside the shrine at all times.
Photography: Photography inside the inner shrine may be restricted; follow posted guidelines.
Offerings: Floral offerings are welcome and can be purchased near the entrance.
Cultural Respect: Be mindful of the dual Buddhist and Hindu significance of this sacred shrine.

name: Asgiriya Maha Viharaya
description: One of the two principal chapters of the Siam Nikaya Buddhist order in Sri Lanka and among the most respected and influential Buddhist monasteries in the country. Asgiriya Maha Viharaya is home to senior Buddhist clergy and plays a significant role in religious, cultural, and ceremonial affairs, including the Kandy Esala Perahera. The complex features traditional Kandyan architecture and well-maintained shrine rooms.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:30 AM - 9:00 AM) to observe morning rituals in a peaceful atmosphere

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: White or light-colored modest clothing is strongly preferred. Shoulders and knees must be fully covered. Remove shoes and hats before entering temple premises.
behavior: Dress Code: White attire is highly preferred as a sign of respect. Sarongs are available if needed.
Footwear: Remove shoes at the entrance before entering any part of the temple complex.
Decorum: Maintain the utmost silence and reverence throughout this highly respected monastic complex.
Photography: Photography is restricted in many areas; always seek explicit permission from monks before photographing.
Monks: Show deep respect to monks at all times. Women should avoid direct physical contact with monks.
Cultural Respect: This is a highly revered active monastic center; visitors should behave with exceptional respect and humility.
entry_requirements: Highest standard of respectful conduct required

name: Montefano, St. Sylvester's Monastery, Ampitiya
description: A tranquil Franciscan monastery nestled in the hills of Ampitiya above Kandy, founded by Italian Franciscan missionaries and named after Montefano in Italy. The monastery is surrounded by lush greenery and offers a peaceful spiritual retreat. It is home to an active monastic community and features a beautiful chapel, gardens, and sweeping views of the surrounding hills.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (9:00 AM - 12:00 PM) on weekdays for a peaceful visit in a serene monastic environment

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress modestly and respectfully. Shoulders and knees should be covered. Avoid casual or revealing clothing out of respect for the monastic community.
behavior: Dress Code: Modest attire covering shoulders and knees is required at all times on the monastery grounds.
Silence: Maintain silence and a contemplative demeanor throughout the monastery.
Photography: Seek explicit permission from the monastery community before taking any photographs.
Respect: This is an active monastic community; be mindful of the friars' routines and do not intrude on private areas.
Visits: Prior arrangement or permission may be required for visits; check with the monastery before attending.
Religious Services: Visitors are generally welcome to attend chapel services; follow the lead of worshippers.

opening_hours: By prior arrangement; generally 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Degaldoruwa Temple
description: A remarkable 18th-century cave temple located on the outskirts of Kandy, renowned for its exceptionally well-preserved and vividly detailed murals depicting scenes from the Jataka tales (stories of the Buddha's previous lives) and daily life in ancient Sri Lanka. The temple is considered one of the finest examples of Kandyan-era painting and is a significant cultural and religious heritage site.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (8:00 AM - 11:00 AM) in natural daylight for the best viewing of the ancient murals

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress modestly with shoulders and knees fully covered. Remove shoes before entering the cave shrine rooms. White or light-colored clothing is preferred.
behavior: Dress Code: Modest attire is mandatory. Sarongs may be available at the entrance if required.
Footwear: Remove shoes before entering the cave temple and shrine areas.
Murals: Do not touch the ancient murals under any circumstances; the oils from skin can cause irreversible damage.
Photography: Photography is allowed but avoid using flash, which can damage the delicate pigments of the ancient paintings.
Decorum: Maintain silence and deep respect inside the cave temple at all times.
Guided Tours: A local guide or the temple caretaker can provide valuable insight into the meaning of the murals.

name: Millennium Elephant Foundation
description: A non-profit elephant welfare sanctuary located in Rambukkana near Kandy, dedicated to the ethical care and rehabilitation of working and retired elephants. The foundation offers visitors an opportunity to interact with elephants, assist with bathing and feeding, and learn about elephant conservation and the cultural role of elephants in Sri Lankan society.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (9:00 AM - 12:00 PM) for bathing and feeding sessions with the elephants

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Wear casual, comfortable clothing that you do not mind getting wet during the elephant bathing session. Closed-toe footwear is recommended.
behavior: Animal Welfare: Follow all keeper instructions when interacting with elephants to ensure the safety of both visitors and animals.
Feeding: Only offer food items provided or approved by the sanctuary staff.
Photography: Photography is encouraged and welcomed throughout the sanctuary.
Ethical Tourism: The foundation prioritizes elephant welfare; avoid any activities not sanctioned by the sanctuary.
Littering: No littering of any kind within the sanctuary grounds.
Children: Children must be supervised by adults at all times when near the elephants.

entry_fee: LKR 2500 for foreign adults; interaction packages available at additional cost

name: Gadaladeniya Rajamaha Viharaya
description: A 14th-century Buddhist temple built by King Bhuvanekabahu IV, situated on a large flat rock with commanding views of the surrounding paddy fields and hills. Gadaladeniya is notable for its distinctive South Indian Dravidian architectural style blended with traditional Sinhalese elements, featuring a gopuram-style entrance tower and beautifully carved stone pillars, making it an outstanding example of medieval Sri Lankan religious architecture.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (7:00 AM - 9:00 AM) for a tranquil visit with golden morning light over the surrounding landscape

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress modestly with shoulders and knees fully covered. Remove shoes before entering the temple. White or light-colored clothing is preferred as a sign of respect.
behavior: Dress Code: Modest attire is mandatory. Sarongs may be available at the entrance if required.
Footwear: Remove shoes before ascending the rock and entering the temple premises.
Stone Structures: Do not touch or lean on the ancient carved stone pillars or decorative elements.
Photography: Photography is generally allowed; avoid flash photography inside the shrine room.
Decorum: Maintain silence and deep respect inside the shrine rooms and throughout the temple complex.
Offerings: Floral offerings are appropriate and can typically be purchased near the temple entrance.

name: Hulu Ganga
description: A scenic river and natural attraction located in the lush countryside near Kandy, offering a serene natural escape surrounded by verdant forests and rural Sri Lankan landscapes. The Hulu Ganga (river) is popular for nature walks, river bathing, and experiencing the unspoiled natural beauty and tranquility of the Kandy region's rural hinterland.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (8:00 AM - 11:00 AM) for the most pleasant weather and clearest river conditions

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Wear casual, comfortable clothing suitable for outdoor and riverside activities. Bring a change of clothes if planning to swim or wade in the river.
behavior: Environment: Do not litter or dispose of any waste into the river or surrounding natural areas.
Swimming: Assess water conditions carefully before swimming; avoid strong currents, especially during and after heavy rains.
Wildlife: Do not disturb local wildlife or damage vegetation along the riverbanks.
Respect: Be respectful of local communities and private properties along the riverbanks.
Photography: Photography of the natural landscape is freely allowed and highly encouraged.
Local Guide: Engaging a local guide is recommended for navigation and to discover the best natural spots along the river.

name: Sri Muthumari Amman Kovil
description: A vibrant and sacred Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Muthumari Amman, a form of the Divine Mother worshipped widely among Sri Lanka's Tamil Hindu community. The temple is known for its colorful gopuram (tower), elaborate rituals, and festive celebrations that draw large numbers of devotees from across the region.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (7:00 AM - 9:00 AM) during puja sessions for an authentic devotional experience

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress very modestly with shoulders and knees fully covered. Avoid wearing leather items inside the temple. Remove shoes before entering. Traditional attire such as a saree or dhoti is appreciated.
behavior: Dress Code: Full modest dress is mandatory. Avoid wearing leather belts or accessories inside the temple.
Footwear: Remove all footwear before entering the temple premises.
Photography: Photography inside the inner sanctum is strictly prohibited; check with temple authorities for other areas.
Decorum: Maintain silence and deep reverence inside the temple at all times.
Rituals: Do not interrupt ongoing puja rituals. Observe respectfully from a designated distance.
Cultural Respect: Non-Hindu visitors are welcome but should be especially respectful of the sacred space and ongoing worship.

name: National Spice Garden of Sri Lanka
description: Located in Matale, the National Spice Garden is a fascinating botanical showcase of Sri Lanka's world-renowned spice heritage. The garden cultivates a rich variety of spices including cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, nutmeg, pepper, and vanilla, offering guided tours that educate visitors on cultivation, processing, and the cultural and medicinal significance of each spice.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (9:00 AM - 11:30 AM) for a guided tour before the afternoon heat and tour group crowds

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual, comfortable clothing suitable for outdoor walking. Light footwear is fine; paths are generally well-maintained.
behavior: Plants: Do not pluck, damage, or remove any plants, spices, or produce from the garden without permission.
Guided Tours: A guided tour is strongly recommended and is often included with entry for the full educational experience.
Photography: Photography is freely allowed throughout the garden.
Purchasing: A spice shop on-site sells authentic locally produced spices and herbal products.
Littering: Do not litter; use designated waste bins throughout the garden.
Respect: Be respectful of garden staff and follow their instructions during the tour.
entry_requirements: Free entry or small guided tour fee

entry_fee: free (guided tour may have a nominal fee)

name: Aluviharaya Rock Cave Temple
description: An ancient and historically significant Buddhist cave temple located in Matale, renowned as the site where the Tipitaka, the sacred Theravada Buddhist scriptures, were first committed to writing on ola leaf manuscripts in the 1st century BC. The temple complex features a series of cave shrines adorned with intricate murals, statues, and vivid depictions of Buddhist cosmology including the realms of hell, making it a unique and spiritually profound destination.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (8:00 AM - 11:00 AM) in natural daylight for the best viewing of the murals and cave interiors

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Dress modestly with shoulders and knees fully covered. White or light-colored clothing is preferred. Remove shoes before entering the cave shrines.
behavior: Dress Code: Modest attire is mandatory. Sarongs may be available at the entrance if required.
Footwear: Remove shoes before entering any of the cave shrine rooms.
Murals & Statues: Do not touch any murals, statues, or sacred artifacts inside the caves.
Photography: Photography is generally allowed; avoid flash photography to protect the ancient paintings.
Decorum: Maintain silence and deep reverence throughout the cave temple complex.
Guided Tours: A local guide can provide detailed explanations of the scriptural murals and historical significance.

name: Riverston
description: A breathtaking highland destination in the Knuckles Mountain Range, Riverston is celebrated for its dramatic landscapes, misty mountain peaks, and sweeping panoramic views stretching across the central highlands. The area offers exceptional trekking trails through cloud forests, waterfalls, and diverse ecosystems, making it one of Sri Lanka's most rewarding adventure and nature destinations.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:30 AM - 9:00 AM) on clear days between January and April for the most spectacular views before clouds settle over the peaks

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Wear warm, layered clothing as temperatures at altitude can be significantly cooler, especially in the morning. Sturdy hiking boots or trail shoes are essential.
behavior: Trekking: Stick to designated trails and do not venture off-path; the terrain can be steep and disorienting in misty conditions.
Wildlife: Do not disturb wildlife. The Knuckles Range is a UNESCO World Heritage-listed biodiversity hotspot.
Littering: Strictly no littering. Carry all waste out of the nature reserve.
Guided Treks: Hiring a certified local guide is strongly recommended, especially for longer hikes.
Photography: Photography is freely encouraged; the landscape offers world-class photography opportunities.
Safety: Inform someone of your trekking plan and expected return time. Weather can change rapidly.

entry_fee: LKR 1500 conservation fee for foreign visitors
opening_hours: Open all day; best accessed from 6:00 AM

name: Bambarakiri Ella
description: A stunning multi-tiered waterfall cascading through the lush forests of the Knuckles Mountain Range near Riverston. Bambarakiri Ella is one of the most scenic and picturesque waterfalls in the central highlands, set within a pristine natural environment and accessible via a rewarding forest trek, making it a favorite destination for nature lovers and hikers.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (7:00 AM - 10:00 AM) after a period of rainfall for the most impressive water flow and freshest forest conditions

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Wear sturdy hiking footwear and comfortable, quick-dry clothing. Expect wet and muddy trail conditions. Bring rain gear as the area receives frequent rainfall.
behavior: Trails: Follow designated trails carefully; the path to the falls can be slippery and steep in places.
Swimming: Exercise extreme caution near the waterfall base; currents can be strong after heavy rainfall.
Littering: Strictly no littering in this protected natural area. Carry all waste out.
Wildlife: Do not disturb wildlife or damage vegetation along the trail.
Photography: Photography is freely allowed and highly rewarding throughout the trek.
Guided Treks: A local guide is highly recommended for navigation through the forest trail.

entry_fee: Nominal fee may apply as part of Knuckles conservation area
opening_hours: Open all day; best accessed from 7:00 AM

name: Nalanda Gedige
description: A remarkable and enigmatic ancient stone temple dating back to approximately the 8th century, located near Nalanda in Matale District. Nalanda Gedige is unique in Sri Lanka as it blends Buddhist and Hindu architectural elements, featuring exquisite stone carvings reminiscent of South Indian temple art. Originally dismantled and reconstructed near a reservoir, it remains one of the most architecturally intriguing historical monuments in the country.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (8:30 AM - 11:00 AM) on weekdays for a peaceful visit with minimal crowds

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual but respectful attire is appropriate given the historical and religious significance of the site. Comfortable walking shoes are recommended.
behavior: Stone Carvings: Do not touch or climb on the ancient stone carvings or structural elements.
Respect: Treat the site with the reverence due to a place of ancient religious and cultural significance.
Photography: Photography is freely allowed throughout the site.
Guided Tours: Engaging a local guide is recommended to fully appreciate the architectural and historical significance.
Littering: No littering of any kind within the site grounds.
Safety: Exercise care around the reservoir embankment adjacent to the temple site.

name: Pitawala Pathana
description: A spectacular high-altitude grassland plateau situated within the Knuckles Mountain Range near Riverston, Pitawala Pathana is a unique patana (montane grassland) ecosystem offering sweeping views across misty valleys and mountain ridges. The plateau is home to endemic flora and fauna and is a key habitat within the UNESCO-listed Knuckles Conservation Forest, making it a premier destination for trekkers and nature enthusiasts.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Very early morning (6:00 AM - 8:30 AM) between January and March for clear skies and the best visibility across the plateau and surrounding peaks

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Wear warm, wind-resistant layers as the plateau is exposed and temperatures drop significantly. Sturdy waterproof hiking boots are essential for the terrain.
behavior: Conservation: This is a protected UNESCO ecosystem. Do not damage, collect, or remove any plants or natural materials.
Trails: Stay on designated trekking paths to avoid damaging the fragile grassland ecosystem.
Wildlife: The patana is home to endemic species including endemic lizards and birds; observe without disturbing.
Littering: Absolutely no littering. All waste must be carried out of the protected area.
Guided Treks: A registered local guide is mandatory for trekking into Pitawala Pathana.
Safety: Weather conditions can change rapidly; carry rain gear and inform someone of your trekking route.

entry_fee: LKR 1500 conservation fee for foreign visitors
opening_hours: Best accessed from 5:30 AM; return before 5:00 PM

name: Sera Ella Waterfall
description: One of the most scenic and accessible waterfalls in the Knuckles Mountain Range area, Sera Ella cascades gracefully through lush tropical forest creating a beautiful natural pool at its base. The waterfall is surrounded by dense greenery and offers a refreshing and picturesque natural retreat, particularly popular with families and nature visitors travelling through the Matale and Knuckles regions.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (8:00 AM - 11:00 AM) after moderate rainfall for the best water flow and a refreshing visit

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Wear comfortable casual clothing and footwear suitable for a short forest walk. Bring a change of clothes if you plan to swim at the base pool.
behavior: Swimming: The natural pool at the base is generally safe for bathing; always assess water depth and currents before entering.
Littering: Do not litter; carry all waste out of the natural area.
Wildlife: Do not disturb wildlife along the trail or near the waterfall.
Photography: Photography is freely allowed and highly encouraged throughout the site.
Noise: Be respectful of other visitors and the natural environment; keep noise levels low.
Safety: Exercise caution on wet and slippery rocks near the waterfall base.

opening_hours: Open all day; best visited during daylight hours

name: Sembuwatta Lake
description: A stunning artificial lake nestled in the misty Elkaduwa highlands amid tea and rubber plantations, Sembuwatta Lake is one of Sri Lanka's most photogenic and romantic natural escapes. Surrounded by lush green hills and forest, the lake offers boat rides, a treetop walk, zip-lining, and serene lakeside walks, making it a popular eco-tourism and leisure destination near Kandy and Matale.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Weekday mornings (8:00 AM - 11:00 AM) for peaceful misty lake views before weekend crowds arrive

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual, comfortable clothing suitable for outdoor leisure. Light footwear is fine for most areas; wear sturdy shoes if planning to use the treetop walk.
behavior: Boat Rides: Life jackets are provided for boat rides; always wear them during the activity.
Activities: Follow all safety instructions from staff for zip-lining, treetop walks, and boat rides.
Littering: Strictly no littering in or around the lake and plantation grounds.
Wildlife: Do not disturb wildlife or damage vegetation around the lake.
Photography: Photography is freely allowed and the scenic lake backdrop makes for exceptional photos.
Respect: Be considerate of other visitors and the natural environment at all times.

entry_fee: LKR 500 for foreign adults; activity fees apply separately

name: Ranweli Spice Garden
description: A well-established and highly regarded spice and herbal garden in the Matale district, Ranweli Spice Garden offers immersive guided tours through cultivated plots of Sri Lanka's finest spices and medicinal plants, including cinnamon, cloves, cardamom, pepper, vanilla, and a wide range of Ayurvedic herbs. The garden provides an educational and aromatic experience that showcases the island's extraordinary spice heritage.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Morning (9:00 AM - 11:30 AM) for a guided tour with knowledgeable staff before the midday heat

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Casual, comfortable clothing and footwear suitable for outdoor walking along garden paths.
behavior: Plants: Do not pluck, damage, or remove any spices or plants from the garden without authorization.
Guided Tours: Guided tours are included and are the primary way to experience the garden; follow the guide's instructions.
Photography: Photography is freely allowed throughout the garden.
Purchasing: An on-site spice shop offers authentic locally grown spices, herbal products, and Ayurvedic preparations.
Herbal Treatments: Ayurvedic demonstrations or treatments may be available; inquire at reception.
Littering: Do not litter; maintain the cleanliness of the garden environment.

name: Wasgamuwa National Park
description: A vast and relatively off-the-beaten-path national park situated on the edge of the central highlands, Wasgamuwa is one of Sri Lanka's premier wilderness destinations. The park is a key habitat for large herds of wild Asian elephants, leopards, sloth bears, sambar deer, and abundant birdlife. Its remote character and dense forests along the Mahaweli and Amban Ganga rivers offer an authentic and uncrowded wildlife safari experience.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6:00 AM safari departure) or late afternoon (3:30 PM) drives for the best wildlife sightings, particularly for elephants near water sources

cultural_guidelines.dress_code: Wear neutral-colored, lightweight clothing such as khaki, olive, or beige to avoid disturbing wildlife. Long sleeves are recommended to protect against insects. Closed-toe shoes are essential.
behavior: Safari Vehicle: Remain inside the safari vehicle at all times unless at a designated viewpoint or rest stop.
Wildlife: Do not feed, provoke, or approach wildlife. Maintain safe distances at all times.
Noise: Keep noise to an absolute minimum to avoid startling animals, especially elephants.
Photography: Photography is allowed and strongly encouraged; long lenses are recommended for wildlife shots.
Littering: No littering of any kind within the national park boundaries.
Guided Safaris: A licensed park guide is mandatory for all safari vehicles entering the park.

entry_fee: USD 25 for foreign adults plus vehicle and guide fees

name: Hulangala Mini World's End Viewpoint
description: A dramatic cliff-edge viewpoint located within the Elkaduwa Plantations, Hulangala Mini World's End offers a breathtaking sheer drop with sweeping panoramic views across the lowland plains and forested valleys far below. Often compared to the famous World's End at Horton Plains, this viewpoint is far less crowded and provides an equally stunning and adrenaline-inducing visual spectacle set within a lush plantation landscape.

crowd_info.best_time_to_visit: Very early morning (6:30 AM - 8:30 AM) on clear days between January and April before cloud cover obscures the view into the valley below
